observed_failure.surface: compiling six current LogicGuard target contracts
observed_failure.prior_local_claim: contract compilation is a read plus deterministic generated-authority write
observed_failure.observed_result: loading each executable FlowGuard contract model also created .skillguard/__pycache__, which the target correctly rejected as mutable former-authority residue

root_cause_backpropagation.supported_cause: the adapter used the standard import loader, whose bytecode-cache write was outside the prior model and tests
root_cause_backpropagation.would_have_failed_if: the compile route had asserted that loading governed model authority leaves no filesystem residue in the target tree
root_cause_backpropagation.new_model_item: a model-authority read used for contract compilation is side-effect-free inside the governed target tree
root_cause_backpropagation.owner_code_contract: flowguard_adapter.load_flowguard_model reads and executes exact source bytes without import-cache writes

generalized_repair.rule: SkillGuard compilation may write only its declared generated current-authority outputs; reading a target-owned executable model must not create bytecode or another undeclared mutable authority.
generalized_repair.observed_regression: loading and compiling a target model leaves no adjacent __pycache__ directory
generalized_repair.same_class_regression: the returned model snapshot and schema validation remain unchanged
generalized_repair.negative_boundary: the repair does not ignore caches during retirement checks, add a target opt-out, or move Guard semantics into SkillGuard

required_revalidation: LogicGuard six-target compilation without control-root caches

revalidation_evidence.flowguard_model_tests: 9 passed, including side-effecting model-authority read rejection

claim_boundary: This review covers only the model-authority read side effect and its SkillGuard compile route. Target-native Guard semantics and target installation closure remain separately owned and evidenced.
